Unseen force.<[silence:600ms]> Accelerating universe.<[silence:600ms]>

The term dark energy names the mystery pushing space apart faster over time—not like a thrown ball slowing down, but like a car pressing the gas pedal harder as it speeds up.<[silence:600ms]> Galaxies drift farther, yet nothing visible explains the shove.<[silence:600ms]>

In 1998, two teams studying distant supernovae expected to find expansion slowing.<[silence:600ms]> Instead, data from Type Ia supernovae (cosmic "standard bombs" with predictable brightness) revealed light stretched redder than physics allowed—proof space itself was stretching faster.<[silence:600ms]> Dark energy now dominates 68% of the universe’s mass-energy, dwarfing atoms and dark matter combined.<[silence:600ms]>

Its push isn’t explosive;<[silence:200ms]> it’s steady, like invisible hands inflating a balloon’s surface while the dots on it spread.<[silence:600ms]> The stronger the pull between galaxies, the more dark energy’s repulsion wins—a tug-of-war where the rope keeps lengthening.<[silence:600ms]>

clean_story:
Unseen force. Accelerating universe.

The term dark energy names the mystery pushing space apart faster over time—not like a thrown ball slowing down, but like a car pressing the gas pedal harder as it speeds up. Galaxies drift farther, yet nothing visible explains the shove.

In 1998, two teams studying distant supernovae expected to find expansion slowing. Instead, data from Type Ia supernovae (cosmic "standard bombs" with predictable brightness) revealed light stretched redder than physics allowed—proof space itself was stretching faster. Dark energy now dominates 68% of the universe’s mass-energy, dwarfing atoms and dark matter combined.

Its push isn’t explosive; it’s steady, like invisible hands inflating a balloon’s surface while the dots on it spread. The stronger the pull between galaxies, the more dark energy’s repulsion wins—a tug-of-war where the rope keeps lengthening.

Three related subjects are cosmic inflation’s first push, Einstein’s discarded constant, and voids growing emptier.
